title: Fed's Powell says it's 'too soon to know' Iran war's impact on economy
text: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell says it's "too soon" to assess Iran conflict's economic impact as oil prices surge above $100 per barrel amid rising tensions.

title: Dow falls nearly 800 points after Powell makes one thing clear: There's no rush to rescue the market
text: U.S. stocks slumped on Wednesday, with the S&P 500 wiping out its gains from earlier in the week, as investors reacted to the Federal Reserve's latest outlook on interest rates and the economy.

title: Powell says he will stay on as head of the Fed until Kevin Warsh is confirmed
text: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell says he will keep serving as head of the central bank if his nominated successor, Kevin Warsh, is not confirmed by the time his term is up in May, as dictated by the law.

title: Powell warns of ‘new inflation' from the Iran conflict as gas prices jump 30%
text: The energy crisis is going to hit you in your 401(k) as well as in your gas tank if Jerome Powell is right.

title: Powell: 'No one' knows what impacts of war will be
text: Fed Chair Jerome Powell said there's uncertainty tied to oil prices amid the U.S.-Iran war. “The U.S. economy is doing pretty well,” Powell said.

title: Here are the five key takeaways from this week's Fed meeting

title: How Historical Oil Surges Influenced Equity Returns In The Following Year (It Doesn't Bode Well)
text: Historical analysis shows that when oil prices double rapidly, the S&P 500 typically suffers significant declines over the following 12 months. Current Middle East tensions and oil weaponization by Iran suggest oil prices could rise further, with $150 per barrel seen as a potential peak.

text: Alan Blinder, Former Federal Reserve vice chairman, joins 'Closing Bell Overtime' to talk what is ahead for the FOMC after Wednesday's decision to leave rates unchanged.

title: PPI Data Signals Firms Turning to AI and Liquidity to Manage Pricing
text: The latest producer price data suggest that inflation is no longer being driven primarily by what businesses pay for goods, but by what those enterprises decide to charge as those goods move through the system. title: Powell Says He Will Remain as Fed Chair Until Successor Is Confirmed
text: Jerome H. Powell, who leads the central bank, also said he would not leave the Fed until a criminal investigation into his handling of renovations was over.

title: Dow falls 700 pts as Fed holds rates, hot PPI fuels inflation fears
text: Wall Street ended sharply lower on Wednesday as investors reacted to the Federal Reserve's decision to hold interest rates steady, rising inflation pressures, and escalating geopolitical risks linked to the Middle East conflict. Major US indexes extended losses following the Fed's announcement and fresh economic data.

title: 3 big takeaways from the Fed's March meeting
text: The Fed opted to hold rates steady at its March meeting, as expected. Chair Jerome Powell talked about jobs, inflation, oil prices, and AI productivity gains.

title: U.S. Crude Oil Inventories Rise by 6.2 Million Barrels
text: Commercial crude-oil stockpiles rose by 6.2 million barrels last week. Analysts had predicted crude stockpiles would decline by roughly 40,000 barrels.

title: It's Starting To Feel A Lot Like 2007
text: Current market conditions closely resemble the pre-crisis environment of 2007 in several primary ways, raising caution for investors. Echoes of the subprime crisis and official reassurances highlight systemic risks that may not be fully appreciated.

title: March Fed Meeting: Policymakers Stay The Course Despite Negative Externalities
text: The March FOMC meeting reflected continued stability despite the multitude of negative externalities. The negative externalities include declining payrolls, rising oil prices, and a highly uncertain geopolitical world outlook.

title: Here are five takeaways from the Federal Reserve meeting
text: Here are five takeaways from the Federal Reserve meeting.

title: Powell: Oil shock 'part of' higher inflation projection for 2026
text: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell explains what factors in the economy are contributing to the higher inflation projection for 2026.

title: Companies See Inflation Inching Up to 2.1%, Atlanta Fed Says
text: Firms' year-ahead inflation expectations increased by 0.2 percentage points to 2.1% in March, according to the Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta's March Business Inflation Expectations (BIE) Survey. That figure is up from the 1.9% recorded in the February BIE Survey but down from the 2.5% recorded in the March 2025 survey.

title: Fed warns Middle East tensions could shake markets as it holds rates
text: US equities held steady on Wednesday after the Federal Reserve left interest rates unchanged, signaling caution amid persistent inflation and growing geopolitical risks in the Middle East. The Fed maintained its target range for the federal funds rate at 3.50%–3.75%, in line with market expectations.

title: Powell: Iran war to increase inflation in the near term
text: Surging oil prices due to the Iran war are expected to increase inflation in the near term, Federal Reserve Chair Powell said. “Near-term measures of inflation expectations have risen in recent weeks, likely reflecting the substantial rise in oil prices caused by the supply disruptions in the Middle East,” the Fed chair said.

title: Crude Awakening
text: Oil price volatility surges as bombs fall in the Middle East Be ready for more wild swings in oil and gas prices. The Cboe Crude Oil ETF Volatility Index (OVX), which measures implied volatility in oil ETF options, estimates the expected volatility of crude oil prices over the next 30 days.

title: Powell delivers his most definitive answer yet on how long he'll stay on as Fed chair
text: Federal Reserve chair Jerome Powell said on Wednesday that he will stay on as “chair pro tem” if his successor Kevin Warsh is not confirmed by mid-May when Powell's term expires.

title: Powell Says He Has No Intention to Leave Fed Until DOJ Probe Over
text: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell says he plans to stay at the Fed until after the Justice Department's investigation is complete during a news conference after the central bank's policy-setting Federal Open Market Committee to leave interest rates unchanged.

title: Fed Chair Powell: If no new Fed chair is confirmed by end of my term I will serve as 'chair pro tem'
text: Federal Reserve chair Jerome Powell answers questions following the FOMC's decision to leave the Federal Funds rate unchanged.

title: Powell Says Fed Rates Are Borderline Restrictive
text: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell says the Fed is in a "difficult situation" and needs to balance current risks during a news conference after the central bank's policy-setting Federal Open Market Committee to leave interest rates unchanged.

text: On today's episode of CNBC Crypto World, crypto markets are on pace to outperform Wall Street despite a Wednesday pullback driven by hotter-than-expected wholesale data. Also, the SEC and CFTC issue joint guidance on the regulatory classification of crypto assets.

title: Fed still expects to cut rates once this year despite spiking oil prices
text: The Federal Reserve's so-called dot plot showed a median estimate of 3.4% for the federal funds rate at the end of 2026, the same as what it had projected at the end of last year. However, a closer look at the overall dot plot showed the balance of projections moved toward fewer reductions.

title: Fed Chair Jerome Powell talks inflation and labor market after leaving rates unchanged
text: Federal Reserve chair Jerome Powell delivers remarks following the FOMC's decision to leave the Federal Funds rate unchanged.

title: Retail Sales Expected To Grow 4.4% This Year, Trade Group Predicts
text: Retail sales are expected to grow by 4.4 percent this year, a stronger pace than in 2025, and well above the 10-year-growth average, according to the National Retail Federation (NRF).

title: U.S. Stock Market Outlook And Levels - Dow Jones Back Below 47,000 As Traders Prepare For High-Impact FOMC
text: US stock benchmarks struggle yet again, suffering from another rise in WTI. The Middle East conflict is escalating and risk-sentiment backs down ahead of a quintessential FOMC meeting.

title: Fed votes to hold rates steady, notes 'uncertain' impacts from Iran war
text: The Federal Reserve on Wednesday voted to hold its key interest rate steady as policymakers navigate their way through higher-than-expected inflation readings, mixed signs on the labor market – and a war.

title: Fed Leaves Rates Unchanged, Projects One Cut in 2026
text: The Federal Reserve leaves interest rates unchanged, but still expects one cut in 2026. The vote was 11-1 to hold the benchmark in a range of 3.5% to 3.75%.

title: Federal Reserve holds interest rates steady
text: Federal Reserve policymakers chose to leave interest rates unchanged at their March meeting amid a softening labor market and uncertainty over the economic impact of the Iran war.

title: FOMC Keeps March Interest Rates Unchanged, Projects Little Change for 2026
text: The FOMC voted to keep interest rates unchanged with just one dissenter, Stephen Miran. "It's not surprising," says Tom White, though a surprise could manifest if there are no cuts for 2026 and even a rate hike in 2027 if inflation pressures worsen.

title: Fed Holds Interest Rates Steady—Warns Iran War May Have ‘Uncertain' Economic Impact
text: President Donald Trump has pressured the Fed and Fed Chair Jerome Powell to cut interest rates more quickly, writing on Truth Social on Wednesday: “When is ‘Too Late' Powell lowering INTEREST RATES?” After the Fed voted to hold interest rates in January, Trump said the U.S. should be paying the “LOWEST INTEREST RATE OF ANY COUNTRY IN THE WORLD” because of the “vast amounts of money flowing into” the U.S. because of his tariffs.
